mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to corporate earnings, increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during periods of rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth decelerates while retail sales accelerates indicates unsustainable consumer borrowing that inevitably reverses, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample regression, or 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals robust economic momentum that overrides rate hike concerns, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below zero during tightening periods, or 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of acceleration episodes within this regime.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index annual growth rate exceeds 2.5 percent, inflation expectations force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, depressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal fails to predict direction (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in total nonfarm payrolls expand aggregate income and corporate revenue bases, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through fundamental earnings growth.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during payroll growth months are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized growth in housing starts and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand that supports near-term equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative out-of-sample predictive coefficient, or 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of synchronized growth episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase signals manufacturing weakness that precedes earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of declining production months within this regime, or the signal loses statistical significance (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward testing.
